{{Short description|Men's national association football team representing Montenegro}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=April 2025}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Montenegro women's national football team}}

| Confederation      = [[UEFA]] (Europe)
| Coach              = [[Mirko Vučinić]]
| Captain            = [[Stevan Jovetić]] 
| Most caps          = Stevan Jovetić (91)
| Top scorer         = Stevan Jovetić (37)

| body1              = FF0000

| leftarm2           = FFFFFF

| rightarm2          = FFFFFF
| shorts2            = FFFFFF
| socks2             = FFFFFF
| First game         = {{Fb|MNE}} 2–1 {{Fb-rt|HUN}}<br />([[Podgorica]], [[Montenegro]]; 24 March 2007)
| Largest win        = {{Fb|SMR}} 0–6 {{Fb-rt|MNE}}<br />{{nowrap|([[Serravalle (San Marino)|Serravalle]], [[San Marino]]; 11 September 2012)}}

The '''Montenegro national football team''' ({{lang-cnr-Latn|Fudbalska reprezentacija Crne Gore}}) has represented [[Montenegro|Montenegro]] in men's international [[Association football|football]] since 2007. It is controlled by the [[Football Association of Montenegro]], the governing body for [[football in Montenegro]]. Montenegro's home ground is [[Podgorica City Stadium]] in [[Podgorica]].

Montenegro is one of the world's youngest international teams, having joined FIFA and UEFA in 2007 following the [[2006 Montenegrin independence referendum]]. The team played its first official international match against [[Hungary national football team|Hungary]] in March 2007.

==History==
{{Update|section|date=April 2025}}

===Formation===
[[File:Montenegro line-up, Czech Rp.-Montenegro EURO 2020 QR 10-06-2019.jpg|thumb|right|Montenegro national team squad in EURO 2020 qualifiers]]
Following the [[2006 Montenegrin independence referendum|independence of Montenegro]] from [[Serbia and Montenegro]], Serbia was set to represent both Serbia and Montenegro in the [[UEFA Euro 2008|Euro 2008]] [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying|qualifying]] stage. However, [[UEFA]] would include Montenegro as a late entry if [[FIFA]] ratified a separate [[Montenegrin Football Association]] before September 2006. Moreover, this did not occur before the competition began.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/internationals/5009872.stm|title=Serbia to take spot in Euro 2008|access-date=11 April 2007|website=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=23 May 2006|archive-date=2 September 2006|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060902134247/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/internationals/5009872.stm|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=26 January 2007|title=Montenegro named UEFA member |url=https://www.uefa.com/news-media/news/0254-0d7b9f45b2f2-79fafee3ff14-1000--montenegro-named-uefa-member/ |access-date=6 January 2024|website=[[UEFA]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Frylan |first=Kevin |date=9 August 2007 |title=UEFA admits Montenegro |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/uk-soccer-uefa-members/uefa-admits-montenegro-idUKL2679416020070126/ |access-date=6 January 2024|website=[[Reuters]]}}</ref>

In October 2006, Montenegro was granted provisional membership of UEFA, with a debate regarding full membership scheduled at a full [[UEFA Congress]] in January 2007.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.uefa.com/uefa/Keytopics/kind=64/newsId=464331.html|title=No decision yet on Gibraltar|access-date=11 April 2007|publisher=[[UEFA]]|year=2006|first=Mark|last=Chaplin|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20061128205508/http://www.uefa.com/uefa/Keytopics/kind=64/newsId=464331.html <!-- Bot retrieved archive --> |archive-date = 28 November 2006}}</ref> Montenegro's first [[FIFA World Rankings|FIFA World Ranking]] was joint 199th place, the last place on the list by default.

===First matches===
[[File:Mirko Vucinic 2012 MNE.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Mirko Vučinić was the first captain of Montenegro]]
On 26 January 2007, the Montenegro FA was granted full membership of UEFA.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.uefa.com/uefa/keytopics/kind=64/newsid=500487.html|title=UEFA to consider 24-team Euro|access-date=11 April 2007|website=[[UEFA]]|year=2007|author=Simon Hart |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070219043936/http://www.uefa.com/uefa/keytopics/kind=64/newsid=500487.html <!-- Bot retrieved archive --> |archive-date = 19 February 2007}}</ref> The team played its first FIFA-recognized friendly match against [[Hungary national football team|Hungary]] on 24 March 2007 at [[Stadion Pod Goricom]] in [[Podgorica]]. Montenegro won 2–1 in front of 12,000 spectators. Striker [[Mirko Vučinić]] scored the country's first goal in the 62nd minute.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://uk.reuters.com/article/worldFootballNews/idUKL2427909120070324|title=Soccer-Montenegro beat Hungary 2–1 in international debut|access-date=11 April 2007|work=Reuters|location=United Kingdom|date=24 March 2007|archive-date=11 January 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090111084524/http://uk.reuters.com/article/worldFootballNews/idUKL2427909120070324}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.uefa.com/competitions/euro/news/kind=1/newsid=519773.html|title=Montenegro take a bow with victory|access-date=11 April 2007|website=[[UEFA]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070604012811/http://www.uefa.com/competitions/euro/news/kind=1/newsid=519773.html <!-- Bot retrieved archive --> |archive-date = 4 June 2007}}</ref> On 31 May 2007, Montenegro was admitted as FIFA's 208th member.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/aboutfifa/federation/bodies/media/newsid=529926.html#blatters+third+term+confirmed|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070603190003/http://www.fifa.com/aboutfifa/federation/bodies/media/newsid=529926.html#blatters+third+term+confirmed|archive-date=3 June 2007|title=Blatter's third term confirmed|access-date=17 October 2007|website=FIFA}}</ref>

==='Golden' era===
On 11 September 2012, Montenegro played against San Marino in [[Serravalle (San Marino)|Seravalle]]. In a one-sided match, Montenegro won 6–0,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.vijesti.me/sport/crna-gora-ubjedljiva-protiv-san-marina-pukla-sestica-oboren-rekord-91254|title=Crna Gora ubjedljiva protiv San Marina - pukla šestica, oboren rekord|access-date=11 September 2012|website=Vijesti|language=cnr|date=11 September 2012|archive-date=19 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180919015520/http://www.vijesti.me/sport/crna-gora-ubjedljiva-protiv-san-marina-pukla-sestica-oboren-rekord-91254}}</ref> the biggest win in the team's history. Montenegro then beat Ukraine 1–0 in [[Kyiv]],<ref>{{cite web| url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/preliminaries/europe/matches/round=258374/match=300182355/index.html?intcmp=livecoverage_header_fwcq_playbyplay_en%7Caccessdate=16|title=2014 FIFA World Cup Brazil {{!}} Matches {{!}} Ukraine-Montenegro|publisher=FIFA|date=16 October 2012|access-date=18 September 2016}}{{dead link|date=October 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> with the only goal scored by [[Dejan Damjanović]].

===Ups and downs===
On 23 February 2014 in [[Nice]], Montenegro were drawn for qualification in [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying Group G]] alongside [[Russia national football team|Russia]], [[Sweden men's national football team|Sweden]], [[Austria national football team|Austria]], Moldova and [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]]. Although Montenegro, opened their campaign with a 2–0 victory against Moldova, they failed to qualify with goalless draw against Liechtenstein, a 1–0 loss against Austria and a 1–1 home draw against Sweden. On 27 March 2015, Montenegro's home match against Russia was abandoned after 67 minutes due to crowd violence, after the Russian [[left-back]] [[Dmitri Kombarov]] was hit by a projectile. The score was goalless and Russia had missed a penalty moments before the match was abandoned. The Russian goalkeeper [[Igor Akinfeev]] was hit by a flare, causing a second 33-minute delay.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.balkaninsight.com/en/article/montenegro-arrests-fans-for-football-match-bowl|title=Montenegro Arrests Fans for Football Violence|website=[[Balkan Insight]]|publisher=Balkan Investigative Reporting Network|date=31 March 2015|access-date=18 September 2016|archive-date=21 August 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160821042626/http://www.balkaninsight.com/en/article/montenegro-arrests-fans-for-football-match-bowl|url-status=live}}</ref> The abandoned match was ruled a 3–0 victory in Russia's favour. Montenegro finished fourth at the end of the campaign and placed 95th on the FIFA ranking list.

==Stadium and facilities==
{{Main|Podgorica City Stadium}}
Montenegro play their home matches at the [[Podgorica City Stadium]] ({{langx|cnr|Stadion pod Goricom|italics=yes}}). The stadium's capacity is 15,230, but international matches reduce this to between 10,700 and 13,000.

===Camp FSCG===
{{main|Camp FSCG}}
The Football Association of Montenegro owns Camp FSCG, a Montenegrin training ground. Built in 2007, the centre has a total area of 54,000 square metres.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.podgorica.me/sport|title=Sportski objekti na teritoriji Glavnog grada Podgorica|website=Podgorica.me|access-date=11 December 2006|language=cnr|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230427074233/https://podgorica.me/sportski-objekti|archive-date=27 April 2023|url-status=live}}</ref> It is located on ''Ćemovsko polje'', a plain located in the outskirts of Podgorica outskirts between the settlements of [[Stari Aerodrom]] and [[Konik, Montenegro|Konik]]. It consists of six pitches with stands and floodlights,<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://fscg.me/istorija-savez/investicije|title=Fudbalski savez Crne Gore|access-date=2016-12-11|archive-date=2014-06-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140605215700/http://fscg.me/|url-status=live}}</ref> and ''House of Football'' – the seat of the Football Association of Montenegro.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://cdm.me/sport/fudbal/video-zavirite-u-novu-kucu-fudbala/|title=VIDEO: Zavirite u novu Kuću fudbala|website=CDM|access-date=11 December 2006|language=cnr|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161220124007/http://cdm.me/sport/fudbal/video-zavirite-u-novu-kucu-fudbala/|archive-date=20 December 2016|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Coaching history==
[[File:Iran vs. Montenegro 2014-05-26 (130).jpg|thumb|225px|[[Branko Brnović]], the longest serving manager in the history of the Montenegro national team]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
! Manager
! Career
! {{Abbr|Pld|Played}}
! {{Abbr|W|Won}}
! {{Abbr|D|Drawn}}
! {{Abbr|L|Lost}}
! {{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
! {{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
! {{Abbr|GD|Goal difference}}
! {{Abbr|Win %|Percentage of games won}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{flagicon|MNE}} {{sortname|Zoran|Filipović}}
|2006–2009
{{WDL|23|8|8|7|for=28|against=31|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{flagicon|Croatia}} {{sortname|Zlatko|Kranjčar}}
|2010–2011
{{WDL|13|6|2|5|for=14|against=11|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{flagicon|Montenegro}} {{sortname|Branko|Brnović}}
|2011–2015
{{WDL|34|11|9|14|for=44|against=50|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;|{{flagicon|Serbia}} {{sortname|Ljubiša|Tumbaković}}
| 2016–2019
{{WDL|26|7|7|12|for=33|against=33|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;|{{flagicon|Montenegro}} {{sortname|Miodrag|Džudović}}
| 2019 {{small|(caretaker)}}
{{WDL|2|0|1|1|for=1|against=4|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;|{{flagicon|BIH}} {{sortname|Faruk|Hadžibegić}}
| 2019–2020
{{WDL|13|5|4|4|for=13|against=16|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;|{{flagicon|MNE}} {{sortname|Miodrag|Radulović}}
| 2020–2023
{{WDL|23|6|4|13|for=22|against=35|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;|{{flagicon|CRO}} {{sortname|Robert|Prosinečki}}
| 2024–2025
{{WDL|13|5|0|8|for=11|against=17|diff=yes}}
|-
|style="text-align: left;|{{flagicon|MNE}} {{sortname|Mirko|Vučinić}}
| 2025–present
{{WDL|5|2|1|2|for=8|against=11|diff=yes}}
|}

==Players==
In [[FIFA|international football]], footballers can normally only play for one national team once they play in all or part of any match recognised as a full international by [[FIFA]]. However, an exception is made in cases where one or more newly-independent states are created out of a former state. Based on current FIFA rules, a footballer will be eligible to play for Montenegro, even if they had previously represented Serbia and Montenegro or any other country and at least one of the following statements applies:<ref>{{Cite web|title=Regulations for the Status and Transfer of Players|url=http://fifa.com/mm/document/affederation/administration/status_transfer_en_25.pdf|url-status=dead|publisher=FIFA|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070922234650/http://fifa.com/mm/document/affederation/administration/status%5ftransfer%5fen%5f25.pdf|archive-date=22 September 2007}}</ref>
*The footballer was born in Montenegro;
*At least one of their parents and/or at least one of their grandparents was born in Montenegro;
*The player has lived in Montenegro continuously for any five-year period.

Due to mixed ancestries, it is likely that a high percentage of the footballers eligible to play for Montenegro will also remain eligible to play for Serbia, and vice versa. However, once they have played for either Serbia or Montenegro in any competitive fixture, they are no longer eligible to play for any other nation.

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#Internationall football|friendly]] matches against [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]] and [[Slovakia national football team|Slovakia]] on 1 and 5 June 2026; respectively.<ref>{{cite news |title=Spisak za mečeve sa Bugarskom i Slovačkom|url=https://fscg.me/vijesti/14197/spisak-za-meceve-sa-bugarskom-i-slovackom|access-date=25 May 2026 |publisher=Fudbalski savez Crne Gore |date=24 May 2026 |language=Serbian}}</ref>

''Caps and goals as of 5 June 2026, after the match against [[Slovakia national football team|Slovakia]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=GK|name=[[Danijel Petković]]|age={{Birth date and age|1993|5|25|df=y}}|caps=29|goals=0|club=[[FK Liepāja|Liepāja]]|clubnat=LVA}}

{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the [[#Current squad|current squad]] due to non-injury issue.
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Not part of the current squad due to injury.
<!-- *<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby.
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team.
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Serving suspension. -->
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
